q: Sound is a sequence of ___ waves which propagates through compressible media.
Sound waves propagate through media such as air or ___.
During their propagation, waves can be reflected, refracted or ___ by the medium.
A relationship between ___ and pressure determines the speed of sound within the medium.
The relationship between density and pressure is affected by ___.
The ___ of the medium determines the rate at which sound is attenuated.
For many media, such as air or water, attenuation due to ___ is negligible.
Speed of a sound wave can also be affected by the density, temperature, and ___ of the medium.
The speed of sound will be ___ for different media.
In a solid medium, the speed of sound is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{Y}{\rho}}$, where $Y$ is ___ modulus.
In a solid medium, $\rho$ is the ___ of the solid material.
In liquids, the velocity of sound is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{B}{\rho}}$, where $B$ is the ___ modulus.
In gases, the velocity of sound is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{\gamma P}{\rho}}$, where $\gamma$ is the ___ constant of gas.
In the gas velocity formula, $P$ is ___ in gas.
Longitudinal sound waves travel ___ in solids than they do in liquids.
Longitudinal sound waves travel faster in liquids than they do in ___.
Young's modulus and the bulk modulus are ___ properties of the medium.
The SI units of Young's modulus and the bulk modulus are ___.
The level of intensity of a sound is measured relative to a reference intensity value ___.
The reference intensity value $I_0$ is taken as ___ $W \cdot m^{-2}$.
The intensity $I_0$ corresponds to the ___ of hearing.
Threshold of hearing is also known as minimum ___ intensity.
The ___ ($dB$) is the unit used for measuring sound intensity.
The intensity level of sound $\beta$ in $dB$ is defined as $\beta (dB) = 10 \log_{10} ___$.
In the intensity level formula, $I$ is the ___ of sound.
In the intensity level formula, $I_0$ is the ___ intensity.
For every $10 dB$ added to the sound level, the loudness of the sound is increased by ___ times.
$120 dB$ sound will be ___ times louder than $100 dB$ sound.
Sound intensity is a direct measure of the ___ reaching an eardrum.
Sound intensity is measured in ___.
Intensity level of a jet plane taking off is ___ $dB$.
Intensity level of loud music is ___ $dB$.
Intensity level of busy street traffic is ___ $dB$.
Intensity level of people talking is ___ $dB$.
Intensity level of a whisper is ___ $dB$.
Intensity level of leaves falling is ___ $dB$.
Intensity level at the threshold of hearing is ___ $dB$.
The intensity of a jet plane taking off is ___ $W \cdot m^{-2}$.
The intensity of loud music is $10^{-2}$ ___.
The intensity of busy street traffic is $10^{___}$ $W \cdot m^{-2}$.
The intensity of people talking is $10^{-7}$ ___.
The intensity of a whisper is $10^{-10}$ ___.
The intensity of leaves falling is $10^{-11}$ ___.
The intensity at the threshold of hearing is $10^{___}$ $W \cdot m^{-2}$.
Sound intensity measured in $W \cdot m^{-2}$ is a direct measure of the ___ reaching an eardrum.
Echoes are produced where a sound wave is ___ from a large and hard surface.
An ___ is a repetition of sound due to the reflection of sound.
The echo can be heard separately if the source of sound is much ___ to the observer than to the reflecting surface.
Reflection of sound obeys the laws of ___.
To reduce the effect of echo in buildings, walls are covered up with ___.
In buildings, floors are covered with ___ and carpets.
Padding, rugs and carpets ___ the incident sound.
Padding, rugs and carpets reduce the amount of sound ___ off wall and floor surfaces.
Echo can also be used to determine the distance $d$ by using formula $v = ___$.
In the echo formula, $v$ is the speed of sound and $t$ is the echo ___.
Some animals such as ___ are known to use echolocation to navigate.
Bats use echolocation to navigate and locate their ___.
___ is the special ability of emitting sounds and interpreting the echoes.
A ___ is a reflection of light or sound waves.
A reverberation is a reflection of waves, or a wide reaching ___ of an action.
An example of a reverberation is the sound bouncing around in a large ___.
___ means the ability to be heard or capability of detecting sounds.
What is the lower limit of human audibility?
What is the upper limit of human audibility?
Which technology is based on echoes and used by ships?
Sonar stands for ___.
A pulse of sound used in sonar is called a ___.
What component in sonar acts as a transmitter?
Infrasound waves are waves with frequencies ___ $20 Hz$.
Infrasound waves are characterized by ___ wavelengths.
Elephants can talk using frequencies as low as ___ $Hz$.
Rhinoceros use frequencies down to ___ $Hz$ to communicate.
Which property allows infrasound to travel longer distances?
Imaging frequencies for medical use are typically in the range of ___ $MHz$.
Which mammal has an audible range up to $150,000 Hz$?
Which mammal has an audible range starting at $10,000 Hz$?
___ frequencies are those that humans cannot hear because they are too low.
The ___ effect is the change in frequency of a wave related to an observer.
Doppler effect is due to the ___ motion between the source and observer.
The Doppler effect is named after the Austrian physicist ___ Doppler.
Christian Doppler described the Doppler phenomenon in ___.
Usually, only the motion of the source and observer directly towards or ___ from each other is considered.
A common example of Doppler shift is the change of frequency (___) heard from a vehicle horn.
In the Doppler equation, $f_o = f_s \left( \frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s} \right)$, $f_o$ is frequency heard by the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $f_s$ is frequency produced by the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $v$ is the speed of sound in the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $v_o$ is speed of the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $v_s$ is speed of the ___.
When source and observer are approaching, $f_o ___ f_s$.
When source and observer are receding, $f_o ___ f_s$.
$+ v_o$ is used when the observer moves ___ the source.
$- v_o$ is used when the observer moves ___ from the source.
$- v_s$ is used when the source moves ___ the observer.
$+ v_s$ is used when the source moves ___ from the observer.
Doppler effect is observed not only in sound but also in ___ radiations.
The wavelength of light from a star observed from earth shifts due to Doppler effect towards the ___ end of the spectrum.
The phenomenon where light shifts toward lower frequency and longer wavelength is called ___ shift.
If a star were approaching the earth, spectral lines would shift towards ___ end.
___ (radio detection and ranging) is a form of echolocation that uses electromagnetic waves.
Radar stands for ___ detection and ranging.
Radar uses ___ waves instead of sound.
Doppler ultrasound is a technique used to examine ___ flow.
The technique of measuring shift in ultrasound reflected by red blood cells is known as ___.
Doppler ultrasound can reveal blockage to blood flow or formation of ___ in arteries.
Doppler ultrasound can provide detailed information on the heartbeat of the ___ during labor.
___ can be used to examine an organ such as the heart.
The ___ of the note (how high it is) is determined by its frequency.
The greater the frequency, the ___ is the pitch.
The loudness of the note depends upon the ___ of the wave that produces it.
The greater the amplitude, the ___ the note.
More ___ is used to produce a larger amplitude.
Quality of a sound is the characteristic which distinguishes the two sounds of the same ___ and same pitch.
Quality of sound is also called ___.
Sounds of same loudness and pitch emitted by two different ___ are distinguished by quality.
The timbre of a sound depends on its ___.
Waveform varies with the number of ___ or harmonics.
Notes produced by the violin are ___ than others because it has more overtones.
The intensity of the sound at a place is the ___ transmitted per second per unit area.
The intensity of sound is measured per unit area held ___ to direction of sound.
The intensity of sound can be expressed as $I = \\frac{1}{2} \\rho v \\omega^2 A^2$ where $\\rho$ is ___ of medium.
The intensity of a wave is directly proportional to the ___ of the amplitude of the wave.
___ is a sensation and hence, unlike intensity, it depends on the observer.
To the human ears, the loudness of the sound is ___.
Normally the greater the ___, the greater the loudness of the sound is.
In the intensity formula, $\\omega$ represents the ___ frequency of sound wave.
In the intensity formula, $A$ represents the ___ of sound wave.
Frequency determines the ___ of a note.
Loudness depends on the ___ of the wave.
Characteristics that distinguish sounds of same loudness and pitch are quality or ___.
The number of overtones or harmonics affects the ___.
Energy transmitted per second per unit area defines ___.
Unlike intensity, loudness depends on the ___.
Higher frequency means a ___ pitch.
Greater amplitude means a ___ note.
Richness of sound from a violin is due to having more ___.
Intensity is the energy transmitted per ___ per unit area.
The area in the intensity definition is held normally to the ___ of sound.
The relation $I \\propto A^2$ means intensity is proportional to the ___ of amplitude.
The loudness of sound is ___ for human ears.
Pitch describes how ___ a note is.
Timbre depends on the number of overtones or ___.
The waveform of a sound determines its ___.
Intensity depends on the ___ of the medium and velocity of the sound wave.
One person may find a sound loud, while another finds it ___.
The intensity formula uses the square of the ___ frequency.
Energy used to produce a larger amplitude results in a ___ note.
Sound notes produced by different instruments are distinguished by their ___.
The higher the frequency, the ___ the pitch.
Amplitude of the wave determines the ___.
Intensity is an energy transmission per second per ___.
Intensity is proportional to the square of the ___.
The sensation that depends on the observer is ___.
Timbre distinguishes sounds with the same ___ and pitch.
Overtones and harmonics determine the ___ of a sound.
Velocity of the sound wave is a factor in calculating ___.
Higher frequency corresponds to a higher ___.
